segments.integrated_caption: Inside a cozy, dimly lit wooden room with rustic walls and a checkered tablecloth, a mischievous gray striped cat, wearing a red hat and scarf, sits writing with a yellow pencil. A low, raspy male voice, belonging to the cat, muses that it's not the clock striking, but someone knocking on the door. A deeper male voice, from the brown dog perched on the fireplace, invites them in. Suddenly, a man with a mustache, wearing a yellow coat and a 'ПОЧТА' hat, enters, carrying a matching bag. He greets them with a booming, theatrical voice, challenging them to guess his identity. The cat's raspy voice confidently declares him 'Admiral Ivan Fyodorovich Kruzenshtern, man and steamboat!' The dog's deeper voice dismisses this, while the cat repeatedly asks 'Who's there?' The man with the mustache, now adopting a higher-pitched, almost childlike voice, reveals himself as 'Postman Pechkin' and announces he's brought the 'Murzilka' magazine, prompting cheers of 'Hooray!' from the cat and dog. He pulls out a yellow-framed object and a white box with black squares, then holds up a black fabric. A red-haired man in a blue coat, holding a white chicken mask and a black bird, gestures animatedly, then interacts with the dog. The dog's voice asks about Uncle Fyodor, and the red-haired man's voice explains that he's in the car and they can't get through because the road is snowed in. The scene transitions to a serene exterior night view of a snow-covered village under a starry sky, with gentle snowfall, as upbeat, folksy instrumental music begins. A signpost reads 'ПРОСТОКВАШИНО 500 м.' A blue car drives through the snow, with the Postman inside, smiling, while the red-haired man, the dog, and the cat are pulled behind on a sled, and the black bird flies alongside, all accompanied by the cheerful music.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a snowy night scene where a gray striped cat, wearing a red Santa hat and scarf, and a brown dog, dressed in a light blue robe, struggle to pull a wooden sled through falling snow, accompanied by the sound of the sled dragging. Over a cheerful, slightly whimsical instrumental tune, an old man's voice with a deep, slightly raspy Russian accent expresses surprise about "sled cats" after mentioning "sled dogs." The dog appears exhausted, while the cat looks determined. The scene shifts to a red-haired man, wearing a blue coat with a fur-lined hood and a red scarf, leaning against a blue car, shivering and looking distressed as heavy snowflakes fall. The old man's voice continues, asking if "sled postmen" have been encountered, to which a second man, with a calm, slightly gruff Russian accent, responds, describing the harsh winter roads and weather, claiming that even "sled academics" are now seen, and he's witnessed it himself. The camera then pans to a distant, snow-covered wooden house with warm lights glowing from its windows, nestled among snow-laden trees. The next shot shows the red-haired woman, smiling as she drives the same blue car, with a black bird with a yellow beak perched on the hood. A young boy's excited voice shouts "Hey, stop! Stop!" then exclaims that the car has gotten out of the pit and he can proceed on his own. Inside the cozy, warmly lit house, a roaring fire burns in the stone fireplace. The cheerful instrumental music transitions into a melancholic acoustic guitar melody, as the gray striped cat, still in his festive attire, joyfully strums a yellow guitar while seated on a wooden stool beside a table covered with a red-and-white checkered tablecloth, a golden samovar, and a bowl of white marshmallows. A male voice sings in Russian, expressing a feeling of being "tamed" and no longer dreaming of seas, as television has replaced nature. The red-haired boy, wearing a yellow sweater, holds a blue cup and stands beside a banner with large blue Cyrillic letters spelling 'НОВЫМ' (NEW), as the black bird flies above. Finally, the brown dog, now wearing a white chef’s hat, stands proudly in front of the fireplace, holding a long string of sausages, his expression cheerful and proud.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a cozy, dimly lit wooden room with rustic charm, featuring a patchwork curtain and a roaring stone fireplace. A man with a mustache, wearing a long yellow coat and a green scarf, sits contemplatively on a wooden chair, holding a black hat, and sings a melancholic Russian song about not being recognized. He then points animatedly off-screen. The focus shifts to a red-haired man with a beard, in a light green tunic, who stands near a dusty, spider-webbed small TV, holding a small black object and gesturing as he comments in a deeper, conversational Russian voice about a "strange test pattern" on the TV. A gray striped cat, named Matroskin, wearing a red Santa hat and scarf, joyfully playing a yellow guitar on a wooden stool beside a table with a red-and-white checkered tablecloth, a golden samovar, and a bowl of marshmallows nearby, responds in a higher-pitched, animated Russian voice, clarifying that the TV's appearance is due to cobwebs, not a test pattern, and that "they have such a pattern on every pot... because they didn't talk." The scene cuts to a brown dog in a white chef’s hat, standing proudly before the roaring fireplace with sausages, who then speaks in a deeper, cheerful Russian voice, announcing "But we've already made up" and explaining it happened "when Uncle Fyodor was pulling out snow," followed by a chuckle. The red-haired man reappears, holding a knife and pointing at the TV, as the cat's voice returns, stating "Because joint labor for my benefit, it unites." The man in the yellow coat reenters, holding a long black bag, looking surprised, and calls out "Matroskin!" as the cat makes a "mrrp" sound. Upbeat accordion music begins. The cat then stops playing, points, and gestures excitedly. The dog reappears, stirring a blue pot. The cat resumes playing, then stops again, pointing and smiling. A stormy night with heavy rain and wind is seen through a window, adding concern as the man in the yellow coat stands near a wooden door, looking worried. The red-haired man continues his repair work on the TV, while the man in the yellow coat sits, pointing and speaking animatedly. The red-haired man gestures by the TV, and the man in the yellow coat points back. The cat points and smiles, the dog stirs its pot, and the cat plays and points again. The red-haired man, over the accordion music, expresses happiness that "everything ended well" and wishes a "pleasant New Year," to which the man in the yellow coat asks, "And you, won't you be with us for it?" The scene ends with the man in the yellow coat looking worried by the door, and the red-haired man gesturing by the TV, all within the warm, rustic wooden room.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with a man with a mustache, wearing a long yellow coat, green scarf, and black beret, standing near a wooden door, holding a black bag labeled 'ПОЧТА'. He appears concerned as he states in a deep, slightly gruff voice, "Don't persuade me. Nowadays, the main decoration of the table, what?" A red-haired boy, in a yellow sweater, sitting at a table with a gray striped cat wearing a red Santa hat, suggests in a higher-pitched voice, "Flowers?" The man dismisses, "Bushes!" He then excitedly gestures and points, adding, "TV, and it shows you cobwebs!" and threatens, "I'll go to my place." The boy, looking surprised, pleads, "Don't leave! We will definitely fix the TV! After all, today they will show our mom on the 'Blue Light'!" The man, now in a cozy wooden room with a red-haired man holding a small black bird, agrees, "Of course, we'll fix it!" as the bird perches on the man's hand and then flies to his head. The man with the mustache continues, "And in the yard there is a beautiful Christmas tree, live and real! Let's decorate it!" The boy excitedly exclaims, "Hooray! Let's!" but then becomes distressed, "Only we don't have toys, we have nothing to decorate with!" The man questions, "How? How nothing?" The boy then suggests, "But we have many old things in the attic! Let's decorate the tree with them!" As upbeat, whimsical, slightly jazzy instrumental music begins, the scene transitions to a snowy night outside, showing a decorated Christmas tree adorned with various ornaments. The camera pans to a brown dog in a white robe and cowboy hat, holding a rifle in the snow. The man's voice returns, formally announcing, "Attention, please! Please make smart faces. Photo hunt on you!" as the dog raises its rifle, aiming forward amidst gentle snowfall.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with a close-up of a gray striped cat, wearing a red Santa hat and scarf, standing beside a young boy in a brown winter coat and fur hat, both under a snow-covered Christmas tree adorned with ornaments, as snow falls gently. They look up with wonder and slight concern, accompanied by whimsical, accordion-like music. The camera cuts to a man in a yellow coat and black beret, peering out of a rustic wooden house window, holding a black bag labeled 'ПОЧТА' (POST). He speaks in a deep voice, asking, "What's the news in Moscow?" The scene returns to the cat and boy, now joined by a tall man with a long nose and beard in a blue winter coat, who, in a higher voice, playfully responds, "Dad and I decided to get a second child." The man in the yellow coat, still speaking in a deep voice, exclaims excitedly, "Oh! What joy! On TV, an uncle with a big mustache gave your mom flowers!" He then declares, "I'll show you this uncle now!" as he holds up a small black TV set displaying a man in a tuxedo and a woman in a white dress. The man in the blue coat laughs, clarifying, "That's not a fellow at all. That's the leader of mom's amateur performance group." The boy, in a child's voice, excitedly exclaims, "Oh! Now our mom is going to sing!" The man in the yellow coat then reveals, "Only this is all in vain. Your TV's sound doesn't work!" The boy, disappointed, laments, "Oh, what a pity we can't hear mom!" The scene shifts to the man in the yellow coat outside with the TV, as the man in the blue coat, the boy, and the cat approach him. He hands the TV to the man in the blue coat, who smiles and places it on the snow-covered ground. The man in the yellow coat gestures toward the TV, which again shows the man in the tuxedo and woman in the white dress. The final shot shows the group—man in blue coat, boy, cat, and a brown dog wearing a cowboy hat—standing together in the snow, looking at the TV with joy and anticipation, as snow continues to fall around them, all set to the whimsical music.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a festive winter scene, as a red-haired woman in a blue ski suit, holding skis and poles, stands before a snow-covered Christmas tree adorned with colorful garlands and ornaments, with snowflakes gently falling. She is heard stating in Russian, "I prepared for this performance for half a year." The scene cuts to a man in a yellow coat and black beret, standing outside a wooden house, holding a small black TV set that displays a woman in a white dress and a man in a tuxedo. He gestures excitedly, pointing at the screen and then at the sky, commenting in Russian, "Wow! Your mom is being broadcast both there and here. How far technology has come." The red-haired woman's voice responds, clarifying, "It's not technology that got here, but I myself got here, on skis. And the concert is recorded on tape." The scene returns to her, now waving cheerfully with a joyful expression, as a chorus of voices shouts "Ura!" (Hooray!) and "Hello, dear friends!" The next shot reveals a group of characters outside the house: a man in a blue coat with a fur collar, a boy in a brown winter coat, a gray striped cat wearing a red Santa hat and scarf, and a brown dog, all waving happily. The red-haired woman's voice adds, "I told you I just can't live without our Prostokvashino." She then stands with the boy and the cat under the Christmas tree, all smiling as snowflakes fall around them, while a cheerful, upbeat, folksy song with a male vocalist and chorus begins, singing about the joys of winter. This music continues as the video transitions to a series of blue title cards with white Cyrillic text, listing the production credits for the film, including screenwriter E. Uspensky, director V. Popov, and composer E. Krylatov, among others, with the final card displaying the copyright notice 'СОЮЗМУЛЬТФИЛЬМ, 1980г.' The entire segment exudes a warm, celebratory, and nostalgic winter holiday cheer, characteristic of a classic Soviet animated film.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a festive winter scene featuring a red-haired woman in a blue ski suit, holding skis and poles, standing before a snow-covered Christmas tree adorned with colorful garlands and ornaments, as snowflakes gently fall. The scene cuts to a man in a yellow coat and black beret, standing outside a wooden house, holding a small black TV set that displays a woman in a white dress and a man in a tuxedo. He gestures excitedly, pointing at the screen and then at the sky, as if presenting the TV to someone inside. The scene returns to the woman in the blue ski suit, who now waves cheerfully, her expression joyful. The next shot reveals a group of characters outside the house: a man in a blue coat with a fur collar, a boy in a brown winter coat, a gray striped cat wearing a red Santa hat and scarf, and a brown dog, all waving happily. The woman in the blue ski suit then stands with the boy and the cat under the Christmas tree, all smiling as snowflakes fall around them. The video transitions to a series of blue title cards with white Cyrillic text, listing the production credits: 'автор сценария Э.Успенский' (screenwriter E. Uspensky), 'кинорежиссер В.Попов' (director V. Popov), 'кинооператор Г.Расулов' (cinematographer G. Rasulov), 'звукорежиссер А.Шер' (sound director A. Sher), 'композитор Е.Крылатов' (composer E. Krylatov), 'текст песен Ю.Энтина Э.Успенского' (song lyrics by Yu. Entin and E. Uspensky), 'исполняют В.Толкунова О.Табаков' (performed by V. Tolkunova and O. Tabakov), followed by credits for animators and artists including 'художники-мультипликаторы: Э.Маслова, М.Рогова, Г.Зеброва' (animators: E. Maslova, M. Rogova, G. Zebrova), and 'художники В.Вышегородцев, Ю.Мещеряков, С.Маракасов, П.Коробаев' (artists V. Vyshnegorodtsev, Yu. Meshcheryakov, S. Marakasov, P. Korobaev). The final card displays the copyright notice 'СОЮЗМУЛЬТФИЛЬМ, 1980г.' (Soyuzmultfilm, 1980). The atmosphere is warm, celebratory, and nostalgic, with a strong focus on winter holiday cheer and the closing credits of a classic Soviet animated film.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a series of static blue title cards displaying white Cyrillic text, listing the film's voice actors, editors, and production crew, all set to a cheerful, slightly old-fashioned Russian children's song with a female choir. The final card shows 'КОНЕЦ ФИЛЬМА' (END OF FILM) and the copyright notice for 'СОЮЗМУЛЬТФИЛЬМ, 1984г.', as the song fades out. The screen then fades to black before transitioning to a bright, animated blue sky with fluffy white clouds, accompanied by a whimsical sound effect and the start of new, playful instrumental music. The colorful, hand-drawn 'СОЮЗМУЛЬТФИЛЬМ' logo, with each letter in a different vibrant color, appears in the center, as a cheerful child's voice exclaims, "Привет! Это Союзмультфильм!" (Hello! This is Soyuzmultfilm!). The voice continues, introducing the channel as having "самыми любимыми мультиками" (the most beloved cartoons) and inviting viewers to "Выбирай, что хочешь смотреть" (Choose what you want to watch). Below the logo, white Cyrillic instruction text appears, reading 'Для активации меню, включите аннотации, нажав значок ⚙️' (To activate the menu, enable annotations by clicking the gear icon). As the clouds gently drift, four white cloud-shaped menus appear sequentially, each containing yellow Cyrillic text: 'Мультики для малышей' (Cartoons for toddlers), 'Мультики для детей постарше' (Cartoons for older children), 'Многосерийные мультики' (Serialized cartoons), and 'Самые известные мультики' (Most famous cartoons). The child's voice announces each menu option as it appears, repeatedly urging viewers to "Выбирай!" (Choose!), and then clarifies, "Если кнопки не работают, включи аннотации в меню плеера" (If the buttons don't work, enable annotations in the player menu). The overall atmosphere is cheerful and child-friendly, designed to engage young viewers with a playful, animated interface.
